title: Space-Time Tradeoffs in Algorithm Design
description: A visual representation of the inverse relationship between the amount of memory space an algorithm uses and the computation time it requires, showing how precomputation (increased space) can lead to faster execution (decreased time).
relationships: Increased Space (Precomputation) leads to Decreased Time
Decreased Space (On-the-fly) leads to Increased Time
The curve shows the inverse relationship T(n) ∝ 1/S(n)
